stories.title: Knicks 105-104 Spurs — Game 2 Recap, New York Leads Series 2-0
stories.angle: KAT 21/13/4 carries Knicks as Brunson goes 7-of-25; Brunson steal + FT in final 13 sec; Wemby misses buzzer 20-footer — Knicks survive to go 2-0

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Brunson: 7-of-25. KAT: 21/13/4. Knicks win anyway.

The steal. The foul. The free throw. The missed buzzer.

105-104. Knicks lead the series 2-0. #Knicks #NBAFinals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Wemby's final 13 seconds in Game 2:

Errant pass off Castle's back — stolen by Brunson.
Foul on Brunson — gave up the lead free throw.
Missed 20-footer at the buzzer.

Knicks 105, Spurs 104. Series: 2-0. #Spurs #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 9:00 AM ET

The Knicks just won their ugliest game of the postseason — and somehow it might be their most impressive.

Jalen Brunson went 7-for-25 from the field. Seven. For. Twenty-Five. That's the kind of shooting night that loses playoff games. And the Knicks still won 105-104.

How? Karl-Anthony Towns stepped up when his backcourt partner went cold. KAT finished with 21 points, 13 rebounds, and 4 assists on highly efficient 8-of-12 shooting. He was the reason the Knicks had any lead to protect going into the final minute.

But the game came down to 13 wild seconds. The Spurs had just gone on a 14-0 run to cut the deficit to one. Victor Wembanyama had the ball. He tried to find a teammate — but his pass hit Stephon Castle in the back. Brunson swooped in for the steal, got fouled going out of bounds, and made the crucial free throw to push the lead to two.

Then came the final shot. Wemby had a clean 20-foot look at the buzzer. It was long. The Knicks won 105-104.

The Spurs were right there. The Knicks barely survived. And now the series heads to Madison Square Garden.
facebook.image_caption: KAT (21 pts, 13 reb) was the Knicks' anchor in Game 2. Brunson's 5 steals sealed it in the final moments. New York leads the NBA Finals 2-0. #Knicks #NBAFinals

article.headline: KAT Carries, Brunson Clutches: Knicks Survive Spurs to Take 2-0 Series Lead

stories.title: NBA Finals Game 3 Preview — First Finals Game at MSG Since 1999
stories.angle: Game 3 June 8 MSG 8:30 PM ET; first Finals game at MSG in 9,845 days; same 1999 Spurs opponent; Knicks now -490 series favorites (opened +164 underdogs)

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Game 3 is Monday at MSG.

The last NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden: 1999. Same opponent. Same city. 9,845 days later.

Knicks lead 2-0. #Knicks #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 11:00 AM ET

Monday night, the NBA Finals comes home to New York.

Game 3 tips off at 8:30 PM ET at Madison Square Garden — and it marks the first NBA Finals game played at MSG in 9,845 days. The last time? Game 5 of the 1999 Finals. Against these exact same San Antonio Spurs.

That series ended with the Spurs winning the championship. This one has the Knicks in control, up 2-0 and now -490 series favorites (they opened as +164 underdogs with San Antonio at -196).

The Spurs need a win desperately. No team in NBA Finals history has come back from 3-0, and the Knicks have been the strongest team in the playoffs all year. But the Spurs have Wembanyama, and Wembanyama has something to prove after last night.

This matchup has everything. Game 3, Monday, June 8, 8:30 PM ET on ABC. Who are you picking?
facebook.image_caption: The NBA Finals returns to the Garden on Monday. Knicks lead 2-0. First Finals game at MSG since 1999 — same Spurs. Game 3, June 8, 8:30 PM ET. #Knicks #NBAFinals

article.headline: The Garden Awaits: NBA Finals Returns to MSG for First Time Since 1999

stories.title: Wembanyama's Brutal 30 Seconds — Turnover, Foul, Missed Buzzer Cost Spurs
stories.angle: Final 13 sec sequence: errant pass off Castle's back stolen by Brunson, foul on Brunson, Brunson makes FT for 105-103 lead, Wemby misses clean 20-footer at buzzer; Wemby 29 pts still not enough

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
29 points. 11-of-21 shooting. And still — the moment Wemby will replay is the final 30 seconds.

Bad pass. Steal. Foul. Missed buzzer.

The Finals do not forgive. #Spurs #NBAFinals

facebook.long_form:
Window: 1:00 PM ET

29 points. 11-of-21 shooting. And still, the moment that will define Game 2 for Victor Wembanyama was a 30-second stretch that went completely wrong.

Here's what happened with 13 seconds left and the Spurs trailing by one:

Wembanyama grabbed what should have been the game-clinching defensive rebound. He turned to pass. But Stephon Castle wasn't looking — and the ball hit him squarely in the back. Jalen Brunson swiped the steal, dove out of bounds, and drew the foul.

Brunson made 1-of-2 free throws. Knicks by two, 105-103.

The Spurs got the ball back. With 2 seconds left, Wembanyama had an open 20-foot jumper — the kind of shot a franchise player makes in that moment. It went long. Game over.

At 22 years old, Wemby is learning the hardest lesson in basketball: brilliance doesn't guarantee victory. Down 2-0, the Spurs need him to answer the call in New York.
facebook.image_caption: 29 pts, 11-of-21 shooting — and still the story of the night was Wemby's final 30 seconds. The NBA Finals is unforgiving. #Spurs #NBAFinals

article.headline: 29 Points and Still Heartbreak: Breaking Down Wembanyama's Brutal Game 2 Finale

- Despite errors, Wemby posted 29 pts, 11-of-21 FG in Game 2.

stories.title: Giannis Trade Saga — Heat Frontrunner as Draft Deadline Looms
stories.angle: Bucks open for business, decision expected before June 23 draft; Heat frontrunner with Bam only off-limits; Portland "unlikely" — Giannis prefers East

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Giannis clock is ticking.

Bucks want a decision before the June 23 draft.
Heat are the frontrunner. Bam is the only untouchable.
Portland is "unlikely" — Giannis prefers the East.

Where does he land? #Bucks #NBA

facebook.long_form:
Window: 4:00 PM ET

While the Finals are playing out in San Antonio, the biggest offseason move in the NBA is quietly building to a boil.

The Milwaukee Bucks are open to trading Giannis Antetokounmpo — and they want a resolution before the June 23 draft. That gives teams less than three weeks to put together a package that makes sense.

Right now, the Miami Heat are considered the frontrunner. Their interest is just as strong as it was at the February deadline. The one condition: Bam Adebayo is untouchable. Everyone else is on the table.

Portland has been mentioned, but that's viewed as unlikely — Giannis reportedly wants to stay in the Eastern Conference, and the Trail Blazers are out West.

Meanwhile, the Bucks' preference is still to keep him if he commits to an extension. This isn't over — but the clock is running.

Where do you think Giannis lands?
facebook.image_caption: Heat are the frontrunner. Draft deadline is June 23. The Giannis sweepstakes are heating up. Where does he land? #NBA #Giannis

article.headline: Heat Remain Frontrunner as Giannis Trade Saga Races Toward Draft Deadline

stories.title: AJ Dybantsa "100%" Sure He's Going No. 1 to Washington in 2026 NBA Draft
stories.angle: BYU wing AJ Dybantsa says he's "100%" certain Wizards pick him No. 1; 25.5 PPG/51% FG this season; -450 odds; draft June 23-24 Brooklyn; CBS Sports notes Wizards haven't committed

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
AJ Dybantsa was asked how certain he is that Washington picks him No. 1 on June 23.

His answer: "100%."

The BYU wing averaged 25.5 PPG this season. He's -450 to go first overall. #NBADraft

facebook.long_form:
Window: 7:00 PM ET

The 2026 NBA Draft is 17 days away — and the player expected to go first overall isn't leaving any room for doubt.

When BYU wing AJ Dybantsa was asked how certain he was that the Washington Wizards would select him with the No. 1 overall pick, he said simply: "100%."

The confidence isn't unfounded. Dybantsa averaged 25.5 points, 6.8 rebounds, and 3.7 assists per game this season while shooting 51 percent from the field. At 6-foot-9 with elite playmaking instincts, he's the prototype NBA teams dream about at the top of the draft. He currently sits at -450 odds to be selected first overall.

There is some intrigue: CBS Sports reported that the Wizards haven't viewed the pick as the lock many assume. Washington officially won the lottery back in May and holds the No. 1 pick on June 23.

Dybantsa says he'll be there. The Wizards have to decide if they agree.
facebook.image_caption: AJ Dybantsa: "100%" he's going No. 1 to Washington. The BYU star averaged 25.5 PPG this season. Draft is June 23 in Brooklyn. #NBADraft #NBA

article.headline: AJ Dybantsa Says He's "100%" Going No. 1 to Washington — The Wizards Are Still Deciding
